# Pinned build tools — LEGACY (guard-free) protobuf toolchain.
#
# These are DEVELOPER/CI tools only — not runtime dependencies. This is one half
# of a dual toolchain (see tools/gen_proto.sh); it generates the pb2 files that
# must run on the library's protobuf>=4.25.1 floor:
#   pypowerwall/local/tesla_pb2.py
#   pypowerwall/tedapi/protobuf/V2024_06/*_pb2.py
# The opt-in V2026_06 set is generated by the newer toolchain in
# tools/requirements-tools-v2.txt instead.
#
# grpcio-tools 1.62.x ships with protobuf 4.25.x, which generates pb2 files that
# do NOT embed ValidateProtobufRuntimeVersion and therefore run on any
# protobuf>=4.25.1 runtime. This is intentionally pinned to keep the generated
# files stable and compatible with the widest user base. (protobuf 4.x is in
# widespread use; 5.x+ introduce a strict runtime version check that would break
# users with other packages that require protobuf<5.) grpcio-tools 1.62.x has no
# wheels for Python 3.13+ — run the LEGACY step under Python 3.11/3.12.
#
# If you upgrade these pins you MUST:
#   1. Run: bash tools/gen_proto.sh
#   2. Update the protobuf floor in requirements.txt and setup.py to match the
#      "Protobuf Python Version" comment in any regenerated LEGACY pb2 file.
#   3. Consider whether the upgrade is a breaking change for library users.
grpcio-tools==1.62.3
protobuf==4.25.1
